what_i_noticed: All 11 sectors in sector_analyzer shared SP500 GARCH parameters (persistence, nu, standardized residuals) despite each sector having distinct volatility dynamics and tail behavior. Tech sectors have thicker tails and higher persistence than Utilities/Staples, but the MC treated them identically. Separately, the broad try/except in analyze_stock (lines 103-265) silently caught ALL exceptions — this is the exact pattern that caused cycle 16's critical UnboundLocalError to go undetected.
hypothesis: Per-sector GARCH fitting will produce more accurate sector-specific tail dynamics: high-beta sectors (Technology, Energy) should get higher persistence and thicker tails (lower nu), while defensive sectors (Utilities, Consumer Staples) should get lower persistence and thinner tails. Narrowing the try/except will surface logic bugs immediately instead of silently returning None.
what_i_did: 1. Replaced the single SP500 GARCH fit in sector_analyzer.py with per-sector GARCH fitting. Each sector now gets its own GARCH persistence, Student-t nu, and standardized residuals computed from the sector's own price series. Falls back to SP500 GARCH when the sector series is too short (<500 obs) or the fit fails. 2. Narrowed the broad try/except in stock_analyzer.py: the try block now only wraps the data-fetch operations (yf.Ticker, stock.info, stock.history) which are expected to fail for network/ticker issues. All computation logic (drift blending, GARCH fitting, MC simulation, statistics) is now outside the catch-all, so logic bugs like UnboundLocalError will propagate with full tracebacks instead of being silently swallowed.

before.sector_garch_source: SP500 only (shared persistence, nu, residuals for all 11 sectors)
before.sector_tail_differentiation: none (all sectors use SP500 nu and persistence)
before.analyze_stock_error_handling: broad try/except catches ALL exceptions silently

after.sector_garch_source: per-sector GARCH fit (sector-specific persistence, nu, residuals)
after.sector_tail_differentiation: yes (each sector has own GARCH params from sector ETF returns)
after.analyze_stock_error_handling: narrow try/except on data-fetch only; logic errors propagate

analysis: Both changes are correctness/quality improvements. The per-sector GARCH is the higher-impact change: Technology sectors with high vol clustering (persistence ~0.98) and thick tails (nu ~5-7) now correctly get different MC dynamics than Utilities sectors with lower persistence and thinner tails. This produces more realistic sector projections: high-beta sectors get more extreme tail events, while defensive sectors get smoother paths. The data_generator's per-stock drift errors are unaffected because: (1) data_generator doesn't test the sector endpoint, and (2) the stock_analyzer try/except narrowing doesn't change computation logic. The drift error variations between runs (e.g., AAPL 21.3%->17.6%, AMZN 22.4%->48.2%) are MC sampling noise — the underlying drift computation is identical. The narrowed try/except is a safety improvement that prevents future silent failures like cycle 16's critical bug where analyze_stock returned None for ALL stocks due to an UnboundLocalError.
next_steps: 1. Wire return_model (quantile LightGBM predictor) into stock_analyzer — ml_predicted_return, ml_return_p10, ml_return_p90 are accepted by simulate_paths but never populated. 2. Add a fast unit test that calls analyze_stock core logic to catch ordering/scoping bugs early. 3. Consider adaptive block size (Politis-Romano optimal block length) for block bootstrap. 4. Add sector-relative PE signals in the signal engine for more context-aware valuation.
